headline: One Psychiatrist for Every 200,000 People
context: Malaysia has approximately 500 psychiatrists serving 33 million people — a ratio seven times below WHO recommendations for upper-middle-income nations.

cards.big: 500 psychiatrists. 33 million people. Do the maths.
cards.sub: Average wait for a public psychiatric appointment in Kelantan: 4 to 6 months.

cards.big: Budget 2026 allocated 1.9% of health spending to mental health.
cards.sub: WHO recommends 5 to 10%. Malaysia spends less per capita than Vietnam.

cards.big: 73% of psychiatrists practise in KL, Penang, and Johor Bahru.
cards.sub: Sabah and Sarawak share fewer than 40 psychiatrists for 6.8 million people.

cards.big: Mental Health Act 2001 has not been amended in 25 years.
cards.sub: It predates social media, the gig economy, and pandemic-era prevalence data.

cards.big: You cannot access a service that does not exist in your postcode.
cards.sub: This is infrastructure, not awareness. Stop running campaigns. Start training doctors.

cards.big: Awareness without capacity is performance. Malaysia needs psychiatrists, not posters.
